words.exampleNL: Hoe is het weer vandaag?
words.exampleEN: How is the weather today?
words.audioHint: The 'ee' sounds like 'ay' in 'say'. The 'w' is between English 'v' and 'w'.

words.exampleNL: De zon schijnt vandaag.
words.exampleEN: The sun is shining today.
words.audioHint: Short 'o' sound, like 'on' in 'gone'.

words.exampleNL: Er komt regen vanmiddag.
words.exampleEN: There will be rain this afternoon.
words.audioHint: The 'g' is guttural. The 'e' is long like 'ay'.

words.exampleNL: Er staat veel wind vandaag.
words.exampleEN: It is very windy today.
words.audioHint: The 'w' is between English 'v' and 'w'. Final 'd' sounds like 't'.

words.exampleNL: Er ligt sneeuw op straat.
words.exampleEN: There is snow on the street.
words.audioHint: The 'ee' is like 'ay' and the 'uw' adds an 'oo' glide.

words.exampleNL: Het is bewolkt, maar het regent niet.
words.exampleEN: It is cloudy, but it is not raining.
words.audioHint: Stress on the second syllable: be-WOLKT.

words.exampleNL: Het is warm buiten. Trek een T-shirt aan.
words.exampleEN: It is warm outside. Put on a T-shirt.
words.audioHint: Similar to English 'warm' but with a shorter 'a'.

words.exampleNL: Het is vandaag erg koud.
words.exampleEN: It is very cold today.
words.audioHint: The 'ou' sounds like 'ow' in 'cow'. Final 'd' sounds like 't'.

words.exampleNL: In de lente bloeien de tulpen.
words.exampleEN: In spring the tulips bloom.
words.audioHint: Short 'e' sound in both syllables.

words.exampleNL: In de zomer gaan we naar het strand.
words.exampleEN: In summer we go to the beach.
words.audioHint: The 'z' is softer than in English. Short 'o' sound.

words.exampleNL: In de herfst vallen de bladeren van de bomen.
words.exampleEN: In autumn the leaves fall from the trees.
words.audioHint: The consonant cluster 'rfst' at the end is challenging. Say each consonant clearly.

words.exampleNL: De winter in Nederland is koud en nat.
words.exampleEN: Winter in the Netherlands is cold and wet.
words.audioHint: Very similar to English. The 'w' is slightly more like 'v'.

words.exampleNL: Er zijn veel wolken aan de lucht.
words.exampleEN: There are many clouds in the sky.
words.audioHint: Short 'o' sound, like 'volk' without the 'v'.

words.exampleNL: De lucht is blauw vandaag.
words.exampleEN: The sky is blue today.
words.audioHint: The 'u' is short. The 'ch' is a guttural sound, then 't'.

words.exampleNL: De temperatuur is vandaag twintig graden.
words.exampleEN: The temperature is twenty degrees today.
words.audioHint: Stress on the last syllable: tem-pe-ra-TUUR.

words.exampleNL: Het is dertig graden buiten.
words.exampleEN: It is thirty degrees outside.
words.audioHint: The 'g' is guttural. Long 'aa' sound. Final 'd' sounds like 't'.

words.exampleNL: Het gaat morgen regenen.
words.exampleEN: It is going to rain tomorrow.
words.audioHint: The 'g' is guttural. Stress on the first syllable: RE-ge-nen.

words.exampleNL: Het sneeuwt in december.
words.exampleEN: It snows in December.
words.audioHint: The 'eeuw' combination: 'ay' + 'oo' + 'w'.

words.exampleNL: Het waait hard vandaag.
words.exampleEN: The wind is blowing hard today.
words.audioHint: Long 'aa' sound followed by 'yen'.

words.exampleNL: De zon schijnt de hele dag.
words.exampleEN: The sun shines all day.
words.audioHint: The 'sch' is a guttural 's-kh'. The 'ij' sounds like 'eye'.

words.exampleNL: Er komt onweer vanavond.
words.exampleEN: There will be a thunderstorm tonight.
words.audioHint: Compound: 'on' (un-) + 'weer' (weather).

words.exampleNL: Ik zag de bliksem in de lucht.
words.exampleEN: I saw the lightning in the sky.
words.audioHint: Short 'i' sound. Stress on the first syllable.

words.exampleNL: Ik hoor de donder in de verte.
words.exampleEN: I hear the thunder in the distance.
words.audioHint: Short 'o' sound. Similar to English 'under' with 'd' in front.

words.exampleNL: Er is veel mist op de weg.
words.exampleEN: There is a lot of fog on the road.
words.audioHint: Pronounced exactly like English 'mist'.

words.exampleNL: Er valt hagel uit de lucht.
words.exampleEN: Hail is falling from the sky.
words.audioHint: Short 'a'. The 'g' is guttural.

words.exampleNL: Er zit ijs op de weg. Rijd voorzichtig.
words.exampleEN: There is ice on the road. Drive carefully.
words.audioHint: The 'ij' sounds like 'eye', plus 's'.

words.exampleNL: Het wordt droog vanmiddag.
words.exampleEN: It will be dry this afternoon.
words.audioHint: Long 'oo' sound. The final 'g' is guttural.

words.exampleNL: Mijn kleren zijn nat van de regen.
words.exampleEN: My clothes are wet from the rain.
words.audioHint: Short 'a' sound. Crisp 't' at the end.

words.exampleNL: Het is een zonnige dag.
words.exampleEN: It is a sunny day.
words.audioHint: From 'zon' (sun) + '-ig' suffix. The final 'g' is guttural.

words.exampleNL: Het is een regenachtige week.
words.exampleEN: It is a rainy week.
words.audioHint: From 'regen' + '-achtig'. Multiple guttural sounds.

words.exampleNL: Neem je paraplu mee, het gaat regenen.
words.exampleEN: Take your umbrella, it is going to rain.
words.audioHint: Stress on the last syllable: pa-ra-PLU.

words.exampleNL: Wat is de weersvoorspelling voor morgen?
words.exampleEN: What is the weather forecast for tomorrow?
words.audioHint: Compound: 'voor' (fore-) + 'spelling' (telling).

words.exampleNL: Nederland heeft een gematigd klimaat.
words.exampleEN: The Netherlands has a temperate climate.
words.audioHint: Stress on the second syllable: kli-MAAT. Long 'aa' sound.

words.exampleNL: Er komt een zware storm aan.
words.exampleEN: A heavy storm is coming.
words.audioHint: Almost identical to English 'storm'.

words.exampleNL: Kijk, er is een regenboog!
words.exampleEN: Look, there is a rainbow!
words.audioHint: Compound: 'regen' (rain) + 'boog' (bow/arc). Final 'g' is guttural.

words.exampleNL: De zonsondergang is prachtig vanavond.
words.exampleEN: The sunset is beautiful tonight.
words.audioHint: Compound: 'zons' (sun's) + 'ondergang' (going under).

words.exampleNL: De lucht is helder vanavond.
words.exampleEN: The sky is clear tonight.
words.audioHint: Short 'e' sound. Clear 'd' in the middle.

words.exampleNL: Het is warm en vochtig vandaag.
words.exampleEN: It is warm and humid today.
words.audioHint: The 'ch' and final 'g' are both guttural sounds.

words.exampleNL: Het is mooi weer. Laten we buiten eten.
words.exampleEN: The weather is nice. Let's eat outside.
words.audioHint: The 'ui' diphthong: say 'ow' with rounded lips.

words.exampleNL: Het regent. We blijven binnen.
words.exampleEN: It is raining. We stay inside.
words.audioHint: Short 'i' sound. Double 'n' is stressed.

words.exampleNL: Welk seizoen vind je het mooist?
words.exampleEN: Which season do you find the most beautiful?
words.audioHint: Stress on the second syllable: sei-ZOEN. The 'oe' sounds like 'oo'.

words.exampleNL: Het vriest buiten. Pas op voor gladheid.
words.exampleEN: It is freezing outside. Watch out for slipperiness.
words.audioHint: Short 'o' sound. Similar to English 'forced' without the 'ed'.

words.exampleNL: Het gaat vannacht vriezen.
words.exampleEN: It is going to freeze tonight.
words.audioHint: The 'ie' is a long 'ee' sound. The 'z' is soft.

words.exampleNL: Het weer is zacht voor de tijd van het jaar.
words.exampleEN: The weather is mild for the time of year.
words.audioHint: Short 'a' sound. The 'ch' is guttural, then 't'.

words.exampleNL: Vanmiddag klaart het op.
words.exampleEN: This afternoon it will clear up.
words.audioHint: Separable verb: 'op' + 'klaren'. In use, 'op' moves to the end.
